topic: 紀伊半島 山岳信仰
query: Mountain shrines in the Kii Peninsula.
reasoning: Rule A applied: get_japan_heritage is not the canonical tool for mountain shrines (browse_advisory explicitly redirects to search_area with q='山岳信仰'), but the data returned (Kongōbu-ji, Kumano Kodō, Negoro-ji) is authoritative and directly relevant to Kii Peninsula mountain worship. Rule B: single root cause is geographic coverage gap — tool scoped to Wakayama only, missing Mie and Nara prefectures which contain critical Kii Peninsula mountain shrine sites (Ōmine-san, Kumano Hongū, Yoshino). This is a Coverage Failure (G). Dims affected: intent_understanding 3 (partial — right region but incomplete peninsula), specificity 3 (named assets visible but half the peninsula missing), constraint_handling 3 (geographic constraint 'Kii Peninsula' = Wakayama+Mie+Nara, only one prefecture served), expression_quality 3 (browse_advisory bulk dominates over shrine data). No featured_cluster, Rule E not triggered. Rule D not triggered (response in en, query in en).
missing_data: Mountain shrines in Mie Prefecture portion of Kii Peninsula (Kumano Hongū Taisha, Nachi Taisha, Hayatama Taisha in their Mie aspects), Nara Prefecture mountain worship sites (Ōmine-san, Yoshino, Kinpusen-ji), Shugendō sites across full peninsula breadth.
improvement_hint: Expand get_japan_heritage to accept region-level queries (e.g. 'Kii Peninsula' → auto-fan-out to Wakayama+Mie+Nara), or route 山岳信仰/mountain shrine queries to search_area with q='山岳信仰' covering all three prefectures. The browse_advisory correctly identifies this gap but the agent did not self-redirect.
